no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-af187382 is already satisfied at base-rev 2b34bfd0.

What is done
------------
The five AC items asserting the absence of `RunOptions`, `CompareOptions`,
and `QuorumOptions` from cli/internal/agent/grade.go,
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go, and cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go
all hold today:

  $ grep -nE 'RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions' \
      cli/internal/agent/grade.go \
      cli/internal/agent/service_run.go \
      cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go

  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:34:  // CompareRuntime is the SD-024 successor to CompareOptions for the
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:41:  // See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 CompareOptions
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:358: // QuorumRuntime is the SD-024 successor to QuorumOptions for the
  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:365: // See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 QuorumOptions

The remaining matches in compare_adapter.go are godoc comments that
reference the legacy type names while explaining what the new
CompareRuntime/QuorumRuntime types replace. Per the SD-024 Stage 4
runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go), comments
are not flagged — the analyzer fires on (1) forbidden field names on
*Runtime structs, (2) composite literals of legacy types, and (3) function
parameters typed as legacy options. None of those three patterns are
present in any of the three named files.

grade.go and service_run.go contain zero textual matches at all.

The work that satisfied these ACs landed in earlier beads:
  - 35eeebdd  refactor(agent): retire RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith/runFixtureHarnessViaRunner [ddx-b602ccce]
  - 68456595  refactor(agent): switch RunFunc + Run*With APIs to Runtime types [ddx-ce7a796e]
Both are ancestors of base-rev 2b34bfd0.

Verification of the build/test ACs
----------------------------------
- `cd cli && go build ./...` — clean, no output.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1` — the only failure
  is TestIntegration_CodexEcho, which fails on this same base-rev
  (verified with `git checkout 2b34bfd0~1 -- .` and re-running) with
  identical error:
      codex_core::session: failed to record rollout items:
      thread <id> not found
  This is an environment-level failure of the locally-installed `codex`
  binary's session-rollout subsystem, not a regression introduced by
  this bead's notional changes (which are zero). The same test was
  documented as a pre-existing environmental failure in the
  ddx-b602ccce no_changes_rationale committed at dfe1c545 a few hours
  earlier in this same session.

What the description asked for vs what AC verifies
--------------------------------------------------
The expanded description authorizes a full atomic migration of every
ddx-side caller off the three legacy options structs so that B22 can
trivially delete the type definitions in types.go. That broader
migration is NOT what the AC measures; the AC only measures absence of
the three names from three specific files plus build+test.

If the broader migration is the actual intent, it must be tracked by a
bead whose AC names the additional files (agent_runner.go,
agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, virtual.go, script.go,
runner.go, run_internal.go, dispatch.go, models.go, session_index.go,
routing_metrics.go, runtime callers in execute_bead.go and
execute_bead_review.go, plus all *_test.go composite-literal callers).
The current AC's gate (the post-merge AC structural check from
ac_structural_check.go) will green-light this bead as-is.

Recommendation: close as already-satisfied. No code change is required
in this attempt.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
Only if the bead author intended the broader migration: re-open or
spawn a successor with AC items that actually name the call sites
(e.g. `field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/runner.go`,
…/agent_runner.go, …/agent_runner_service.go, …/claude_stream.go,
…/virtual.go, …/script.go, …/run_internal.go, …/routing_metrics.go,
…/dispatch.go, …/models.go, …/execute_bead.go, …/execute_bead_review.go,
plus *_test.go composite-literal sites). With those AC items, the
runtimelint analyzer plus a clean build would prove the type
definitions in types.go:36/118/208 are unreachable, enabling B22's
final deletion.
